macro_segments.title: Introduction to the Fourth Perek and the Ben Pakua: Fetal Status and Shechita of the Mother

micro_segments.title: Opening remarks on the fourth perek and its theoretical nature compared to practical issues of kashrut
Introduction to the ben pakua: when a fetus in the womb is slaughtered with the mother
Mishna: animal in difficult childbirth where fetus extends a limb and pulls it back, then mother is slaughtered
Mishna: if fetus's head comes out, it is considered born even if pulled back in
Case of fetus dismembered inside mother's womb versus detached spleen or kidney
Explanation of the principle: fetus is not truly a limb of mother; cutting fetus does not make it ever min hachai

macro_segments.title: Rav Yehuda's Position: The Forbidden Limb and the Concept of Chutz Lemchitzato

micro_segments.title: Rav Yehuda in Rav's name: the limb that sticks out becomes forbidden
Deriving the principle from basar basadeh tereifa: meat that leaves its zone becomes forbidden
Application to sacrificial meat and the concept of leaving one's designated space
Explanation of Rav's position using the field metaphor: the limb once out of bounds cannot come back

macro_segments.title: Gemara's Challenges to Rav's Position: The Mishna's Implication and the Braita Evidence

micro_segments.title: Gemara questions the Mishna: does it permit only the fetus or also the limb?
Gemara suggests the Mishna's real point is not just the head criterion but something about the limb
Evidence from Mishnayot about when birth is considered: human firstborn and placenta as proof
Comparison between humans and animals regarding prozdor and the significance of the face

macro_segments.title: The Placenta as a Sign of Birth and Evidence from Braita on Fetal Limbs

micro_segments.title: Mishna: placenta emerging proves fetus was born and is forbidden

macro_segments.title: Rav Nachman bar Yitzchak's Resolution: The Makom Chataach (Place of Cutting)

micro_segments.title: Explanation: the line neither in nor out becomes permitted when limb pulled back

macro_segments.title: The Placenta as a Sign of Birth and Evidence from Braita on Fetal Limbs (continued)

micro_segments.title: Braita paralleling the Mishna: fetus extends limb and pulls back, then mother slaughtered—permissible
Braita: if mother slaughtered while limb out, then limb pulled back—forbidden
Braita: if limb cut off while out, it is ever min hachai; if cut while mother slaughtered, different status

macro_segments.title: Rav Nachman bar Yitzchak's Resolution: The Makom Chataach (Place of Cutting) (continued)

micro_segments.title: Rav Nachman bar Yitzchak: the Mishna teaches only about the makom chataach—the boundary line
Application to the braita with hoof and hooves: each teaches about the boundary and uncloven hooves

macro_segments.title: Ulla's Disagreement with Rav: The Limb Becomes Permitted When Pulled Back

micro_segments.title: Ulla in Rebbi Yochanan's name: the limb itself becomes permitted when pulled back
Rav Yehuda challenges Ulla citing that both Rav and Shmuel say the limb remains forbidden
Ulla's response: reverence for Rav and Shmuel but supporting Rebbi Yochanan's view

macro_segments.title: Rebbi Yochanan's Principle: Basar Basadeh Applies Only to Sin Offerings, Not General Cases

micro_segments.title: Rebbi Yochanan: the basar basadeh tereifa principle applies only when Torah explicitly states it for chatat
Rebbi Yochanan's conclusion: fetuses and their limbs are not like sacrifices, so pulling limb back permits it
Derivation from Moshe's inquiry about the se'ir hachatat on the eighth day of Milu'im

macro_segments.title: Braita Evidence for Basar Basadeh and Final Weighing of Positions

micro_segments.title: Braita: basar basadeh tereifa applies to all meat that leaves its zone, analogous to tereifa

macro_segments.title: Rebbi Yochanan's Principle: Basar Basadeh Applies Only to Sin Offerings, Not General Cases (continued)

micro_segments.title: Application to maaser sheni and bikurim: non-sacrificial items taken out and returned become permitted

macro_segments.title: Braita Evidence for Basar Basadeh and Final Weighing of Positions (continued)

micro_segments.title: Conclusion: Gemara weighs in favor of Rav's position that the limb remains forbidden

topical_tags.term: shechita of mother works for fetus
ever min hachai (limb from living animal)
ubar yerech imo (fetus like limb of mother)
basar basadeh tereifa (meat in field forbidden)
Rebbi Yochanan's position: limb permitted when pulled back
